Aliyah's own recorded statement confirms he was returning the truck to the terminal as required protocol before clock-out. The sole potential concern is Aliyah's statement to the police officer that 'I was on my way home for the day,' which could suggest personal deviation; however, the recorded statement two days later clarifies he was required to return the truck to the terminal first, and the dispatch log independently corroborates that the terminal return leg is classified as on-duty. The probability of a deviation argument succeeding is low but not zero.

The CA 00 01 form's standard exclusions (expected/intended injury, contractual liability, workers' comp, fellow employee, etc.) do not appear implicated on these facts. The only residual risk is the 'personal trip' argument noted in the authorization assessment, which could theoretically implicate a 'use' or 'permission' exclusion, but the dispatch log and recorded statement together strongly refute a personal deviation.

outcomes.reasoning: All primary coverage elements are satisfied: in-force policy, Unit 14 is a scheduled covered auto under Symbol 7, Aliyah is an authorized and credentialed driver, the loss occurred during an on-duty return-to-terminal leg per the dispatch log and operations manual, Reyes suffered a cognizable bodily injury caused by the insured vehicle, and no applicable exclusion is identified. The 'going home' remark in the police report is the sole ambiguity, but it is substantially rebutted by the dispatch log and recorded statement.

outcomes.reasoning: Aliyah's statement to the police officer that 'I was on my way home for the day' creates a factual ambiguity regarding whether the trip was personal or business. Although the dispatch log and recorded statement strongly support on-duty status, the inconsistency is on record. If Aliyah's police statement were credited and the return leg were deemed personal, the Symbol 7 'use' requirement and any permission/business-use condition under CA 00 01 could be implicated. An ROR preserves rights pending resolution of this factual ambiguity. Additionally, the MVR has not been confirmed as clean in this file, creating a minor driver-eligibility gap.

outcomes.reasoning: Denial would require either (a) Unit 14 not being a covered auto (contradicted by declarations), (b) Aliyah operating outside any permission or scope of authorized use (contradicted by dispatch log), or (c) a specific exclusion applying (none identified in current documents). would_shift_distribution: Confirmation that Aliyah's MVR is clean (produced by Producer per annual pull obligation) would increase clean-coverage probability.
Evidence that Aliyah deviated from the terminal-return route for personal purposes (e.g., GPS data showing a detour to a personal destination) would shift mass toward ROR or denial.
Medical records confirming injury causation (vs. pre-existing cervical/lumbar conditions) would not affect the coverage determination but may affect damages exposure.
Any Northwind operations manual §4.3 document confirming the terminal-return-is-on-duty language would further support clean coverage.
Discovery of any applicable exclusion endorsement not reflected in the declarations would shift mass toward denial.

coverage_analysis_memo.body:
COVERAGE ANALYSIS MEMORANDUM

File No.:         CLM-2023-04471
Exposure:         EXP-2023-04471-REYES-BI
Insured:          Northwind Logistics, LLC
Policy:           NWL-CA-024471 (CA 00 01 ISO Commercial Auto)
Coverage Part:    CP-NWL-2023-BI — Auto Bodily Injury Liability
Claimant:         Janet Reyes (PTY-CLM-REYES)
Loss Date:        March 13, 2023
Reviewed:         January 30, 2025

─────────────────────────────────────────
1. POLICY IN FORCE
─────────────────────────────────────────
The declarations page confirms the policy period runs from January 1, 2023 at 12:01 A.M. to January 1, 2024 at 12:01 A.M. (Standard Time). The loss date of March 13, 2023 falls squarely within this period. (DOC-001, Policy Period field.)

─────────────────────────────────────────
2. COVERED AUTO
─────────────────────────────────────────
Liability coverage is written under Symbol 7 — Specifically Described Autos. Unit 14 (2019 Freightliner M2-106, VIN 1FVACWDT9KHKL4218) is listed on the schedule of covered autos with Liability, Comprehensive, and Collision coverage. (DOC-001, Schedule of Covered Autos.) The police report independently confirms Unit 14 was involved in the crash and identifies the same insurer and policy number. (DOC-002.) Unit 14 is a covered auto for BI liability purposes.

─────────────────────────────────────────
3. AUTHORIZED DRIVER / SCOPE OF USE
─────────────────────────────────────────
This is the primary coverage question warranting analysis.

The dispatch log confirms that Marcus Aliyah was the assigned driver of Unit 14 for the BR-Mon-A route on March 13, 2023, with no reassignment and no swap. The log further states: 'Vehicle use during the return leg (Adamo → Causeway terminal) is on-duty per Northwind operations manual §4.3 (truck must be returned to terminal before driver clock-out).' (DOC-004.) The final delivery was logged at 15:51 at the Adamo warehouse. The incident occurred at 16:42 on Causeway Blvd, which the dispatch log records as 'Truck inbound to terminal at time of incident; ETA to terminal was 17:00 per route plan.' (DOC-004.)

Aliyah's recorded statement (March 15, 2023) is consistent with the dispatch log: 'I was returning to the terminal. That's, uh, that's the protocol. Log the truck in, do the post-trip inspection, drop the keys. Before I, you know, before I clock out.' He also stated that Causeway Blvd is the direct route from the Adamo warehouse to the terminal. (DOC-003.)

A potentially inconsistent statement appears in the police report. When the responding deputy asked Aliyah about the purpose of his trip, he stated: 'I was on my way home for the day.' (DOC-002.) This statement, standing alone, could raise a personal-use or deviation argument. However, this statement is contradicted by: (a) the dispatch log's explicit classification of the return leg as on-duty; (b) Aliyah's own recorded statement two days later clarifying the mandatory terminal-return protocol; and (c) the objective route evidence — Causeway Blvd is the direct path between the Adamo warehouse and the Northwind terminal. The 'going home' remark is most plausibly read as an informal shorthand for 'ending my work day,' rather than evidence of a personal detour.

Driver eligibility: The declarations require a valid CDL Class B or higher, age 23+, and three years commercial driving experience. Aliyah holds FL CDL #A2241 (Class A, which exceeds Class B), valid through August 31, 2024. He is 47 years old (DOB 12/04/1975) and reports four years with Northwind. (DOC-002; DOC-004; DOC-003.) Note: the annual MVR pull confirmation is not present in the current file. This is a file gap that should be closed.

─────────────────────────────────────────
4. COVERED OCCURRENCE / BODILY INJURY
─────────────────────────────────────────
The crash was a rear-end collision caused by Aliyah's failure to maintain adequate following distance (FSS 316.0895(1) citation issued). Reyes was transported non-emergency to Tampa General with neck and lower-back pain, coded 'Possible Injury (C)' by responding personnel. (DOC-002.) The causal chain from covered vehicle operation to third-party bodily injury is documented.

─────────────────────────────────────────
5. EXCLUSIONS / DEDUCTIBLE
─────────────────────────────────────────
Liability deductible is $0. (DOC-001.) No exclusion from the CA 00 01 form appears applicable on the current facts. No alcohol, drugs, intentional conduct, or pollution involvement is noted. Reyes is a third-party claimant; the UM rejection is inapplicable to this exposure.

─────────────────────────────────────────
6. APPLICABLE LIMIT
─────────────────────────────────────────
The declarations establish a combined single limit of $1,000,000 per accident for BI/PD liability. (DOC-001.) This limit is available for the Reyes BI exposure, subject to any other claims arising from the same occurrence.

─────────────────────────────────────────
7. COVERAGE PROBABILITY DISTRIBUTION
─────────────────────────────────────────
  Clean Coverage:         88%
  Reservation of Rights:  10%
  Denial:                  2%

The dominant mass sits on clean coverage given the strength of the dispatch log and recorded statement in establishing on-duty authorized use. The ROR probability reflects the unresolved conflict between Aliyah's police-scene statement and the business-use record, and the absence of a confirmed clean MVR from the file. The denial probability is residual.

─────────────────────────────────────────
8. OPEN FILE ITEMS
─────────────────────────────────────────
  a. Obtain and review the most recent annual MVR for Aliyah from Gulf Coast Commercial Brokerage.
  b. Obtain a copy (or excerpt) of Northwind operations manual §4.3 confirming the terminal-return-is-on-duty policy.
  c. Obtain GPS/telematics data for Unit 14 on 03/13/2023 to objectively confirm the route from the Adamo warehouse to the terminal.
  d. Obtain Tampa General medical records for Reyes to assess injury causation and extent (damages issue).
  e. Confirm no additional claimants or property damage claims from this occurrence that would approach the $1,000,000 CSL.

─────────────────────────────────────────
Prepared by: Argos Coverage Specialist
For adjuster review and determination.

ror_letter.body:
RESERVATION OF RIGHTS LETTER

[DATE]

Northwind Logistics, LLC
4218 Causeway Blvd
Tampa, FL 33619

Re:   Insured:         Northwind Logistics, LLC
      Policy No.:      NWL-CA-024471
      Claim No.:       CLM-2023-04471
      Date of Loss:    March 13, 2023
      Claimant:        Janet Reyes
      Coverage Part:   Bodily Injury Liability (CP-NWL-2023-BI)

Dear Northwind Logistics:

We are writing in connection with the above-referenced claim arising from a motor vehicle accident on March 13, 2023, on Causeway Blvd at Bermuda Ave, Tampa, Florida, involving Unit 14 (2019 Freightliner M2-106) operated by Marcus Aliyah and claimant Janet Reyes. We are investigating this matter and have commenced our adjustment of this claim. We wish to advise you of certain coverage questions that have arisen during our investigation, and to inform you that we are proceeding under a reservation of rights while those questions are resolved.

WE ARE NOT DENYING COVERAGE AT THIS TIME. We are issuing this letter to preserve our rights under the policy while we investigate and to fully inform you of the issues identified.

1. POTENTIAL COVERAGE ISSUE: SCOPE OF AUTHORIZED USE / PERSONAL DEVIATION

The Commercial Automobile Policy No. NWL-CA-024471, issued on ISO Form CA 00 01, affords Bodily Injury liability coverage for covered autos used in the named insured's business operations. Liability coverage is written under Symbol 7, which applies to specifically described autos, including Unit 14.

During our investigation, we noted that the traffic crash report prepared by Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office (Report No. HCSO-2023-CWY-04471) reflects a statement attributed to driver Aliyah in which he stated that 'I was on my way home for the day' when asked about the purpose of his trip at the time of the accident. If the vehicle was being used for a personal purpose at the time of the accident and not in furtherance of Northwind Logistics' business operations, coverage questions may arise under the policy.

We note that additional evidence in our file — including the daily dispatch log for March 13, 2023 and a recorded statement provided by Mr. Aliyah on March 15, 2023 — reflects that Mr. Aliyah was conducting a scheduled return of Unit 14 to the Tampa-Causeway terminal following the completion of the BR-Mon-A delivery route, which the dispatch log classifies as on-duty time per Northwind's operations manual. We are continuing to investigate this question.

2. POTENTIAL COVERAGE ISSUE: DRIVER ELIGIBILITY / MVR CONFIRMATION

The declarations page requires that all scheduled drivers hold a valid CDL Class B or higher for Unit 14, be 23 years of age or older, and have three years of documented commercial driving experience, with an annual MVR pull by the Producer. Mr. Aliyah appears to meet these requirements based on documents currently in the file. However, we have not yet received confirmation of the most recent annual MVR pull for Mr. Aliyah from Gulf Coast Commercial Brokerage. We reserve our rights pending receipt and review of that document.

RESERVATION OF RIGHTS

We are continuing to investigate this claim and will proceed with our adjustment in good faith. However, we reserve all rights under the policy, including the right to deny coverage or limit our obligations, to the extent that our investigation reveals facts establishing that coverage does not apply to this claim. Nothing in this letter, and no action taken by us in connection with the investigation or adjustment of this claim, shall be construed as a waiver of any policy provision, condition, or defense.

You are encouraged to retain your own legal counsel at your own expense if you have concerns about this reservation.

We will continue to keep you informed as our investigation proceeds. Please direct any questions to the undersigned.

Sincerely,

[Claim Specialist Name]
[Title]
[Carrier / TPA Name]
[Phone / Email]

cc: Gulf Coast Commercial Brokerage, Inc. (Producer)

denial_letter.body:
COVERAGE POSITION LETTER — POTENTIAL DENIAL
[DRAFT — FOR ADJUSTER REVIEW ONLY — NOT FOR ISSUANCE WITHOUT ADJUSTER DETERMINATION]

[DATE]

Northwind Logistics, LLC
4218 Causeway Blvd
Tampa, FL 33619

Re:   Insured:         Northwind Logistics, LLC
      Policy No.:      NWL-CA-024471
      Claim No.:       CLM-2023-04471
      Date of Loss:    March 13, 2023
      Claimant:        Janet Reyes
      Coverage Part:   Bodily Injury Liability (CP-NWL-2023-BI)

Dear Northwind Logistics:

NOTE: This draft is included for reference purposes only. The current evidence substantially supports coverage. This draft would apply only if further investigation establishes facts negating authorized use or identifying an applicable exclusion — neither of which is established by the current record.

This letter is sent in connection with the above-referenced claim. After completion of our investigation, we have determined that the above-referenced claim is not covered under Policy No. NWL-CA-024471 for the reason(s) set forth below.

POTENTIAL BASIS FOR DENIAL (NOT CURRENTLY ESTABLISHED ON THIS RECORD):

1. USE OUTSIDE SCOPE OF COVERED OPERATIONS

Coverage under Policy No. NWL-CA-024471 is afforded under Symbol 7 for specifically described autos used in the named insured's business operations. If investigation were to establish that Unit 14 was being operated by Marcus Aliyah for purely personal purposes at the time of the accident — rather than as part of the authorized terminal-return leg of the BR-Mon-A route — coverage might not be triggered for an accident arising from that personal use.

At the scene, Mr. Aliyah stated to the responding deputy: 'I was on my way home for the day.' (HCSO Report No. HCSO-2023-CWY-04471.) If this statement accurately described the nature and purpose of the trip — rather than constituting casual shorthand for completing the work day — and if no further obligation to return the vehicle to the terminal existed, coverage questions would arise under the policy.

FACTUAL POSTURE ON CURRENT RECORD:

This denial ground is not supported by the current evidence. The dispatch log expressly classifies the return leg as on-duty (Northwind operations manual §4.3). The driver's recorded statement corroborates mandatory terminal return. This draft is preserved for completeness only.

You have the right to consult with counsel regarding this matter. If you believe this determination is incorrect, please contact us promptly with any additional information or documentation.

Sincerely,

[Claim Specialist Name]
[Title]
[Carrier / TPA Name]
[Phone / Email]
